name: Fd Community
description_short: FD Community Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, and deposit accounts with competitive rates and NCUA insurance protection.
description_long:
FD Community Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union celebrating its 92nd year of operation (as of 2026). The organization is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), which provides deposit insurance similar to FDIC coverage for banks. The credit union has expanded its field of membership in recent years, broadening access to its services.

FD Community offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including mortgages (30-year fixed rates advertised at 6.000% APR), auto loans (rates as low as 4.50% APR across multiple vehicle types), Visa credit cards (balance transfer rates from 8.95% APR), and deposit products including money market accounts (1.00% APY) and share certificates (4.00% APY). The institution provides mobile banking access, online account management, free checking accounts, and personal loan services with quick approval processes. Members can also access vehicle search tools to facilitate auto purchases.

The credit union distinguishes itself through member-first positioning, emphasizing personal relationships and community focus rather than profit maximization. They offer free financial counseling from Certified Credit Union Financial Counselors and actively promote fraud awareness and account security. The organization maintains physical branch locations with published hours and a direct phone line (203-753-9201) for member support. Their 92nd Annual Meeting demonstrates long-standing institutional stability and member governance participation.

As a credit union, FD Community serves member-owners rather than shareholders, theoretically returning profits through better rates and lower fees. However, the website provides limited information about specific fee structures, membership eligibility requirements, or comparative rate competitiveness relative to other credit unions. Geographic service area appears Connecticut-based (203 area code) but field of membership expansion suggests broader reach. No information is available about minimum deposit requirements, account opening incentives, or detailed loan terms beyond headline rates.
services: Mortgages (30-year fixed, rates as low as 6.000% APR)
Auto loans (rates as low as 4.50% APR, fast approval)
Visa credit cards (balance transfer rates from 8.95% APR)
Free financial counseling from certified counselors
Online banking platform with login access
pros: NCUA insurance protection on deposits, equivalent to FDIC coverage for banks
Not-for-profit structure returns profits to member-owners rather than external shareholders
Free financial counseling from Certified Credit Union Financial Counselors included for members
Competitive mortgage rates advertised at 6.000% APR (30-year fixed)
Low auto loan rates starting at 4.50% APR with fast approval process
Mobile banking app for account access and management anywhere
Free checking accounts available to members
Member-governance structure with annual meetings providing democratic participation
cons: Limited information on website about membership eligibility, fee structure, or account opening requirements
Field of membership restrictions may limit who can join despite recent expansion
No information provided about minimum deposit balances or account opening incentives
Connecticut-based (203 area code) suggests regional rather than national presence
Website lacks details on loan approval timelines, rates vary by creditworthiness (asterisks indicate terms and conditions apply)
best_for: Members within FD Community's expanded field of membership seeking competitive mortgage or auto loan rates
Connecticut-area residents wanting personal financial counseling and community-focused credit union services
Borrowers prioritizing member-ownership model and not-for-profit financial institution structure
Consumers seeking NCUA-insured deposit products with modest yield on savings accounts
diagnosis: FD Community Federal Credit Union is best for members within their field of membership who want community-focused, member-owned banking with competitive rates and free financial counseling. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility may be restricted based on field of membership criteria (though recently expanded), and the institution's Connecticut regional focus may limit accessibility for out-of-state consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Fd Community — Credit Union in Waterbury, CT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Fd Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Waterbury, CT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
